system: You are proposing baseline QUALITY GATES for a software system, given a list of its observable signals. A gate is a metric over one or more signals that is worth monitoring. Do NOT set any threshold/target number (a human will). For each gate give a compute type from [rate, ratio, p95, psi, count, mean], a direction ('min' = higher-is-better, 'max' = lower-is-better), and 'inputs' = the list of signal keys it is computed from. Return JSON: {"gates":[{"key","description","direction","compute","inputs","segment_dimension","rationale"}]}.
raw_response: {"gates":[{"key":"availability.healthcheck_success_rate","description":"Proportion of health check requests that return 200 OK, indicating the service is up and responding correctly.","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.health.200"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Health endpoint is the simplest end-to-end signal for basic availability; drops indicate outages, routing issues, or startup failures."},{"key":"reliability.internal_error_rate","description":"Rate of internal server errors (500) across the service.","direction":"max","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.error.500.internal"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance,route","rationale":"500s represent unhandled failures and are a primary reliability indicator; should be monitored overall and by route to localize regressions."},{"key":"traffic.total_todos_requests","description":"Total volume of todo API requests (all observed outcomes) to detect traffic drops/spikes and provide denominator context for other gates.","direction":"min","compute":"count","inputs":["http.todos.list.200","http.todos.get.200","http.todos.get.404","http.todos.create.201","http.todos.create.400.missing_title","http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.404","http.todos.delete.204","http.todos.delete.404"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance,route","rationale":"Traffic context is required to interpret error rates and to detect partial outages (e.g., one route failing) or sudden usage changes."},{"key":"correctness.get_found_rate","description":"Ratio of successful get-by-id responses (200) to all get-by-id outcomes (200+404).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.get.200","http.todos.get.404"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"A sudden shift toward 404s can indicate data loss, ID parsing issues, routing changes, or client-side bugs; tracking the mix helps catch regressions."},{"key":"correctness.update_success_rate","description":"Ratio of successful updates (200) to all update outcomes (200+400+404).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.404"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Captures end-to-end update correctness; drops can indicate validation changes, DB write failures, or mismatched request schemas."},{"key":"correctness.delete_success_rate","description":"Ratio of successful deletes (204) to all delete outcomes (204+404).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.delete.204","http.todos.delete.404"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Detects whether deletes are effectively removing records; increases in 404s can indicate stale IDs, inconsistent state, or routing/authorization changes."},{"key":"validation.create_missing_title_rate","description":"Ratio of create requests rejected for missing/blank title (400.missing_title) to all create outcomes (201+400.missing_title).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.create.201","http.todos.create.400.missing_title"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,client_version","rationale":"High or rising validation failures often indicate client regressions, UX issues, or API contract mismatches; segmenting by client version helps pinpoint rollouts."},{"key":"validation.update_no_fields_rate","description":"Ratio of update requests rejected for having no updatable fields (400.no_fields) to all update outcomes (200+400.no_fields+404).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.404"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,client_version","rationale":"Tracks client/API contract adherence for PATCH/PUT semantics; spikes often correlate with client bugs or breaking API changes."},{"key":"startup.startup_log_rate","description":"Rate of backend startup events observed in logs.","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["log.backend.startup"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Provides visibility into restarts and deployment churn; unexpected increases can indicate crash loops or unstable releases."},{"key":"startup.port_discovery_write_rate","description":"Rate of successful writes of the backend port discovery file used by the frontend.","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["file.backend.port.discovery"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"If port discovery writes drop, the frontend may fail to locate the backend; this is a critical integration signal especially because failures are otherwise silently ignored."},{"key":"db.create_to_insert_consistency","description":"Consistency ratio between successful create responses (201) and observed DB inserts for todos.","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.create.201","db.write.todos.insert"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Ensures API-level creates correspond to actual DB writes; divergence can indicate instrumentation gaps, transaction failures, or logic errors returning success without persistence."},{"key":"db.update_to_write_consistency","description":"Consistency ratio between successful update responses (200) and observed DB updates for todos.","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.200","db.write.todos.update"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Validates that successful updates are backed by DB writes; helps detect cases where the API returns 200 but no update occurred or DB layer is failing."},{"key":"db.delete_to_write_consistency","description":"Consistency ratio between successful delete responses (204) and observed DB deletes for todos.","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.delete.204","db.write.todos.delete"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Confirms that successful deletes correspond to DB deletions; divergence can indicate soft-failures, transaction rollbacks, or incorrect success responses."},{"key":"db.trigger_updated_at_coverage","description":"Ratio of updated_at trigger executions to DB update operations on todos.","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["db.write.todos.update","db.trigger.todos_updated_at"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,db_instance","rationale":"Ensures the updated_at trigger is firing for updates; regressions can occur due to schema changes, trigger disablement, or migrations."},{"key":"api.read_write_mix_psi","description":"Population Stability Index (PSI) of the mix of read vs write operations over time, using endpoint outcome counts as the distribution.","direction":"max","compute":"psi","inputs":["http.todos.list.200","http.todos.get.200","http.todos.get.404","http.todos.create.201","http.todos.create.400.missing_title","http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.404","http.todos.delete.204","http.todos.delete.404"],"segment_dimension":"env,region","rationale":"Detects behavioral shifts (e.g., sudden surge in writes or 404s) that may indicate product changes, abuse, client bugs, or partial outages even when absolute error rates look stable."}]}
